_schema.purpose: Single source of truth for filter-artifact identity. Every physical file that claims to represent a filter body belongs here. Human names collide across artifact classes ('Talking Hedz' names four non-equivalent files); this registry makes 'what object is this?' impossible to get wrong.

fields.internal_id: Unique, code-safe snake_case ID. Never reuse across artifacts.
fields.display_name: Human-authoring name. NOT unique — may collide.
fields.source_class: heritage_designer_xml | calibration_re | raw_p2k_skin | compiled_v1 | rust_const_rom | canonical_render
fields.truth_status: authoring_source | runtime_truth | runtime_compile | theoretical_reference | reference_render | deprecated
fields.path: Repo-relative path. WAV sets list the glob.
fields.render_chain: Ordered pipeline from this artifact's stored data to a final audio sample.
fields.sample_rate: Authored sample rate in Hz, or null if format-agnostic.
fields.boost: Post-cascade scalar boost, or null if the format does not store one.
fields.derived_from: internal_id this artifact was produced from, or null for root sources.
fields.notes: Brief caveat — what this artifact is NOT, if the name is misleading.

_schema.naming_policy: Never refer to a filter by display_name alone in code comments, test docs, or commit messages. Use internal_id.

artifacts.render_chain: 30-int authoring grid → type 1/2/3 firmware compile (pyruntime/heritage_coeffs.py) → 6-stage kernel coeffs → DF2T cascade
artifacts.notes: MorphDesigner XML preset. Q axis is collapsed by construction — NOT what the real E-mu runtime does for the Talking Hedz patch.

artifacts.render_chain: per-stage {pole_freq_hz, radius, val*} → SOS cascade → AGC → × boost → float32 WAV
artifacts.notes: Authoritative RE'd runtime model. 5-stage captured form (real engine throws out one stage). Use this when asking 'what does the real Talking Hedz filter sound like'.

artifacts.render_chain: compiled-v1 DF2T kernel coeffs → FilterEngine (cascade → AGC → × boost → DC blocker)
artifacts.notes: Plugin-loadable compile of cal_talking_hedz. Nulls against the cal_Talking_Hedz_*.wav set.

artifacts.render_chain: per-stage {a1, r, val*, flag} → SOS cascade → AGC → × boost → float32 WAV
artifacts.notes: Raw theoretical 6-stage P2K skin, byte-identical to upstream trenchwork_clean. NOT what the real engine runs — the real runtime discards one stage and hard-converts the last captured stage (see cal_talking_hedz). Plugin convention still maps 'P2K index 013' → 'Talking Hedz' (hotswap tool doc).

artifacts.render_chain: compiled-v1 DF2T kernel coeffs → FilterEngine (cascade → AGC → × boost → DC blocker)
artifacts.notes: Plugin-loadable compile of raw_p2k_013. The committed hedz_rom.rs is baked from THIS file, not from cal_talking_hedz — choose deliberately when comparing against canonical WAVs.
Hardcoded plugin default. Regenerate with `python tools/bake_hedz_from_p2k.py`. Previously baked from md_hedz (morph-only, stale).

artifacts.render_chain: cal_talking_hedz → resample 44100→39062.5 → SOS cascade @ 39062.5 → per-sample AGC → × 4.0 → resample 39062.5→44100 → float32 WAV @ 44100
artifacts.notes: Null-test reference for compiled_talking_hedz. Dry source: C:/Users/hooki/trenchwork_clean/ref/bypassed-pinknoise.wav (per ref/canonical/PROVENANCE.json).

artifacts.render_chain: raw_p2k_013 → resample 44100→39062.5 → SOS cascade @ 39062.5 → per-sample AGC → × 4.0 → resample 39062.5→44100 → float32 WAV @ 44100
artifacts.notes: Null-test reference for compiled_p2k_013 / rom_hedz. Same dry source as cal set. Distinct from canonical_wav_cal_talking_hedz — the two sets measure different filters despite the shared 'Talking Hedz' name.

null_test_pairs.rationale: compile-path null: does the compiled-v1 of the calibration_re model reproduce the Python raw-stage render?
plugin-default null: does the baked Rust rom reproduce the P2K raw-stage canonical render?

forbidden_comparisons.reason: different source filter (raw_p2k_013 vs cal_talking_hedz). The display name matches but the underlying DSP is different — raw_p2k_013 is the theoretical 6-stage, cal_talking_hedz is the captured 5-stage runtime.
